audits.body:
Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/ttf-ms-win10.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-apmylcsc...

Analyzing ttf-ms-win10 AUR package (working in /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-apmylcsc) with qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 from https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines a package that bundles Microsoft Windows 10 TrueType fonts. The `source` array uses `file://` URLs pointing to local files (e.g., `file://arial.ttf`, `file://license.rtf`), which means no network-based downloads occur during the `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` phase. The only code executed at parse time is in the global scope, which consists solely of variable assignments (e.g., `pkgname`, `_ttf_ms_win10` arrays, `sha256sums`) and a redefinition of `DLAGENTS`. 

The `DLAGENTS` line:
```bash
DLAGENTS=("file::/usr/bin/echo ${BOLD}${RED} Unable to find %u, please read the PKGBUILD ${ALL_OFF}" $DLAGENTS[@])
```
overrides the default handler for `file://` sources to display an error message if a source file is missing. This executes `echo` with color variables (likely defined elsewhere in the build environment), but no malicious command substitution or external execution beyond formatting the message occurs. This is a user-friendly warning, not a backdoor.

No `pkgver()` function is present, so no dynamic version computation occurs. The entire script during sourcing consists of safe declarations and assignments. There is no command injection, network exfiltration, or execution of untrusted payloads during the parsing and source-fetching phase covered by the two commands.

Thus, running `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` and `makepkg --printsrcinfo` is safe, as it only sources the PKGBUILD and handles local file references without executing any harmful operations.
</details>
<summary>No remote sources or unsafe code in parse phase</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No remote sources or unsafe code in parse phase[/]

Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/ttf-ms-win10.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-mvj8vl6f...

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines a set of Microsoft Windows 10 fonts to be packaged and includes file paths and checksums for them. The `source` array uses `file://` URLs, which reference local files that must be placed in the build directory by the user. This means no network downloads are performed during the sourcing or source-fetching phase of `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`. The `DLAGENTS` override only affects download agents for other protocols and outputs a warning message if a file is missing, using shell color variables (`${BOLD}`, `${RED}`, `${ALL_OFF}`) for formatting. These are benign print statements and do not execute any malicious code.

There is no command substitution, `pkgver()` function, or other executable code in the global scope outside of static array definitions and variable assignments. The checksums are explicitly listed, with only the final entry set to 'SKIP', which corresponds to `license.rtf`—a standard and expected practice when the file's content may vary or isn't distributed directly (e.g., due to licensing). Since no dynamic code execution occurs during the parsing of this PKGBUILD or during source retrieval for the given `makepkg` flags, there is no risk of unintentional code execution, data exfiltration, or remote payload fetching at this stage.

All functions (`_package`, `package_*`) are defined but not executed under `--noprepare` and `--nobuild`, so they are out of scope for this narrow gate.
</details>
<summary>
No remote downloads or malicious code in sourcing phase.
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No remote downloads or malicious code in sourcing phase.[/]

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines all sources as `file://` URIs (local files) and uses a custom download agent that merely prints an error message if a file is missing. No network downloads are triggered. The global scope contains only variable assignments and array definitions; no commands like `curl`, `wget`, `eval`, or command substitutions that execute anything. There is no `pkgver()` function. The `prepare()`, `build()`, and `package()` functions are not invoked by the requested commands (`--nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`). Therefore, sourcing the PKGBUILD and fetching the sources (which are local copies) is safe.

The `sha256sums` array includes legitimate checksums and a single `SKIP` for the license file, which is typical for local files. No malicious behavior is present in the scopes that execute.
</details>
<summary>Only local file sources; no dangerous top-level code.</summary>
</security_assessment>

data.reasoning: PKGBUILD uses only local file sources with harmless DLAGENTS override; no build/install functions present; fonts are non-executable data files.
coverage_issues: Both models failed to review actual source files (font TTF files) due to makepkg failure
Potential .install hooks not reviewed (though none appear present)
Critical gap: makepkg failure blocked all file reviews despite PKGBUILD safety
false_negatives: Models missed verifying checksums against actual font files (though low-risk for non-executables)
Lack of review for license.rtf content (minimal risk, but should be checked for non-standard clauses)
learnings: Tool should handle file:// source failures more gracefully - audit available files (PKGBUILD/.install) even when sources missing
Prioritize review of package() function and .install hooks when present
Improve error recovery: when makepkg fails, still audit existing files and note missing sources
Add explicit check for checksum verification routines in PKGBUILD
Treat non-executable data files (fonts/images) as lower priority than build scripts
